submit.title: Chief Transformation Officer (CTO) – AI, Digital & Enterprise Transformation
submit.jd_text: At Innovation Consulting Group (ICG), together with a portfolio of subsidiaries and specialized entities in the Middle East, such as HFS Research and Nityo Infotech,we partner with leading organizations across the Globe to drive measurable transformation across strategy, technology, people, and operations. Our combined capabilities span enterprise applications, cloud modernization, Artificial Intelligence (AI), automation, digital engineering, cybersecurity, enterprise platforms, innovation, outsourcing, and large-scale transformation advisory. Together, we deliver a comprehensive, multi-entity transformation ecosystem, enabling organizations to modernize operations, deploy intelligent digital capabilities, strengthen resilience, and accelerate transformation initiatives aligned with Vision 2030 and regional agendas. ICG, supported by its broader group ecosystem, is evolving into an AI-first transformation platform, enabling enterprises to move from vision → architecture → execution → measurable impact. About The role We are seeking a Chief Transformation Officer (CTO) to lead the strategic development, governance, and growth of ICG’s enterprise transformation portfolio across the MENA region . The CTO will act as the executive leader responsible for the firm’s transformation ecosystem across ICG and its subsidiaries , orchestrating capabilities across multiple entities while ensuring a unified market approach. This role combines transformation leadership, solution architecture, and commercial ownership, with a strong focus on shaping and closing large-scale, multi-domain transformation and outsourcing deals ($100M–$300M) . What You Will Do Enterprise Transformation Strategy & AI Leadership Define and lead the enterprise transformation strategy across MENA Drive adoption of AI-powered solutions including generative AI, intelligent automation, and AI agents Align offerings with key transformation priorities across customer, operations, and technology Identify and shape large-scale transformation and outsourcing opportunities Position ICG and its broader group ecosystem as a leading transformation partner in the MENA region Service Line Leadership & Organizational Oversight Lead the enterprise transformation organization across ICG and its subsidiaries Oversee core domains: AI, Cloud, Cybersecurity, Digital Platforms, and Strategy & Innovation Drive cross-entity collaboration to deliver integrated transformation programs Ensure alignment across technology, process, organization, and product Commercial Enablement & Strategic Deal Leadership Lead and support closure of large, complex transformation deals ($100M–$300M) Oversee Solution Sales Experts across all domains Drive cross-selling strategies and multi-year transformation programs Engage C-level stakeholders and build long-term partnerships Lead deal structuring and integrated solution design Offering Development & Consulting Frameworks Develop and standardize transformation

submit.title: Digital and Cyber - Digital Strategy and Realisation - AI Strategy - Director
submit.jd_text: Why PwC At PwC, your growth is our priority. We fuel potential through coaching, feedback, and challenging work that helps you build a career path as unique as you are. You’re not just joining a team—you’re becoming part of a movement that’s shaping the future. Here, you can be yourself, work with purpose and boldness, and collaborate with talented people to deliver solutions that create real impact for clients and communities. Powered by inclusive teams, cutting-edge technology, and truly meaningful work, you’ll find endless opportunities to grow, lead, and go further. Together, we set the future in motion. Consulting LoS At PwC Consulting, we lead with insight and deliver with purpose. You’ll work alongside experts across industries to design and deliver forward-looking solutions in areas such as strategy and enterprise transformation, operational excellence, digital innovation, cyber resilience, and risk management. Our reimagined operating model brings together sector-aligned, solution-led teams empowered to move fast, think boldly, and create real impact. Whether transforming finance, enabling digital change, or shaping national visions, you’ll be part of a collaborative, forward-thinking community—built to grow with you and the world around us. BU Description The Digital & Cyber Business Unit brings together deep technology expertise and bold thinking to help organizations thrive in a connected, data-driven world. We support clients in reimagining how they operate, serve, and grow—by harnessing the power of digital products, emerging tech, cloud, data, and cyber resilience. Whether shaping enterprise-wide transformation or enabling targeted innovation, we work at the intersection of strategy, technology, and trust—delivering scalable solutions that empower clients to move faster, think smarter, and lead with confidence. Job Summary We are seeking an experienced and visionary AI Strategy expert to lead engagements that define and deliver impactful artificial intelligence strategies, both at national and enterprise levels. The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in AI, machine learning, and generative AI (GenAI), along with a strong grasp of business strategy, data ecosystems, and emerging technology governance. Role This role requires an individual who can bridge the gap between strategic ambition and technological execution. The successful candidate will have the ability to: Formulate national AI visions and strategies, shaping policy, capability building, and innovation ecosystems; and Design enterprise AI operating models and roadmaps, driving adoption across functions and value chains. The role involves working closely with senior executives and public sector leaders to translate AI potential into measurable impact, advising on operating models, governance frameworks, talent strategies, and data and technology foundations required to enable AI at scale. The candidate should have strong interpersonal skills, who not only enjoy

submit.title: Director of Retail Operations
submit.jd_text: We are a leading multinational retailer based on GCC. We are looking for an experienced Director of Retail Operations to organize and oversee the operations of our stores. You will assume responsibility for the company’s overall success by setting targets, supervising managers, and ensuring that they are attained. The Operations Director is expected to execute company strategies by maximizing store profitability. You will have control over store expenses, protect company assets, and manage overall store standards and processes for customer service excellence, merchandising and inventory management, retail operations, and ensure that team participation is consistent and well managed. Effective operations directors are skilled in managing diverse operations. They have a strategic mindset and are excellent leaders. Excellent financial and operational planning abilities are essential for this role. The goal is to ensure that our company meets and exceeds the expectations of business development and efficiency. Duties and responsibilities Provides leadership to the team and oversees retail activities. Hires, trains, and oversees management team performance. Full P/L responsibility. Sets objectives for retail teams to ensure a high standard of service provided in the store. Ensures structure is provided for the retail teams in KPIs, policies and procedures, trading feedback processes, trading calendars, and controlling retail costs. Reviews and analyzes sales and operational records and reports; uses data to project sales, determines profitability and targets, and identifies potential new markets. Prepares and implements the county budget; monitors and approves expenses. Collaborates with managers to develop sales goals for each location, monitors progress toward those goals and develops strategies to attract and retain customers. Develops marketing programs and promotions to increase sales and/or introduce new products. Identifies opportunities for improvement; designs and implements training, strategies, policies, goals, and other resources to maximize productivity and morale. Schedules and directs regular sales meetings and events to share information, set and revise goals, and increase morale. Oversees warehouse operations in conjunction with the Supply Chain Manager. Manages inventory by monitoring inventory levels and planning stock supply to ensure the availability of the full line of products. Maintains knowledge of the market, competition, and best practices and trends in sales techniques and strategies. Conducts performance evaluations that are timely and constructive. Handles discipline and termination of employees in accordance with company policy and labor law. Performs other duties as assigned. Requirements Strong verbal and written communication skills. Excellent sales and customer service skills, with proven negotiation skills. Excellent math skills to draft and implement budgets and conduct data analysis. Excellent supervisory and lead

submit.title: Executive Director, Information Technology
submit.jd_text: Role Overview: Ensure the efficiency and effectiveness of the Information Technology sector by overseeing the implementation of IT policies, regulations, and guidelines. The role is responsible for meeting the organization’s needs for IT infrastructure and applications while enabling continuous performance improvement, supporting digital transformation, and implementing the organization’s IT strategy. Key Responsibilities: • Develop strategic plans and programs for the IT department and oversee their implementation after approval. • Supervise affiliated administrative units and coordinate their activities to ensure operational integration and optimal utilization of available resources. • Oversee the development and implementation of IT policies across the organization. • Lead the development of the digital transformation roadmap and apply best practices in IT governance and management while ensuring the expected benefits from IT projects are achieved. • Contribute to the development and update of the vision, mission, and strategic objectives for information and communications technology within the organization. • Prepare periodic progress reports on IT projects, monitor performance indicators, identify obstacles, assess risks, and coordinate with the Project Management Office. • Oversee the preparation and execution of the five-year IT strategy. • Supervise the preparation of technical specifications and requirements for IT projects in coordination with relevant departments within the organization. • Oversee the development of the annual operational plan and estimated budget for the department and monitor its implementation. • Supervise the development and continuous improvement of IT operational processes and procedures. • Monitor national ICT initiatives and programs and leverage relevant opportunities for the organization. • Oversee studies related to IT service needs and requirements. • Design and develop the enterprise IT architecture supporting the organization’s operations and ensure proper documentation. • Oversee the development and implementation of IT service development programs and initiatives within the organization. • Manage and operate IT services in line with recognized best practices and standards. • Develop and manage internal and external digital portals, e-services, and smart applications, and establish frameworks and tools for business intelligence and data analytics. Qualifications & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Database & Network Design or a related field, with a minimum of 12 years of experience. Alternatively, 10 years with a Master’s degree or 8 years with a PhD in a relevant discipline.

submit.title: Head of Digital Product Delivery (Mobile App)
submit.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
submit.jd_text: A confidential, customer-focused organisation is seeking a Head of Digital Product Delivery (Mobile App) to lead the strategy, prioritisation, and delivery of a flagship consumer mobile app and its associated customer-facing digital product capabilities. This is a senior, business-led role with end-to-end accountability for turning strategic ambition into tangible digital outcomes. The role is intentionally hands-on designed for a leader who combines strong product thinking with disciplined programme and delivery execution, and who uses modern delivery practices to ship outcomes. The Role As Head of Digital Product Delivery (Mobile App) , you will act as the senior business owner for the mobile app. You will define what the product is, how it evolves, and how it delivers value for customers and the business, working in close partnership with Digital Technology teams and external delivery partners. While not responsible for hands-on software development, this role is deeply embedded in product, programme, and delivery leadership. You will personally drive planning, prioritisation, sequencing, dependencies, milestones, and day-to-day execution to ensure initiatives are delivered with quality, pace, and measurable impact. Key Responsibilities Define and own the product vision and strategic roadmap for the mobile app, aligned to customer experience, commercial objectives, and brand standards Translate high-level business goals into clear, prioritised product initiatives and releases Act as the single point of accountability between business stakeholders and delivery teams Lead the end-to-end delivery lifecycle, from concept and planning through build, launch, and ongoing optimisation Partner closely with Digital Technology teams and external agencies to ensure delivery against agreed scope, timelines, and quality standards Establish and maintain a predictable release cadence, with strong stakeholder visibility and confidence Balance short-term delivery priorities with longer-term platform scalability and evolution Champion a customer-first mindset, working closely with UX/UI resources to deliver intuitive, high-quality digital experiences Ensure product quality across functionality, performance, accessibility, and stability Define and track product success metrics, using data, analytics, and feedback to continuously improve adoption, engagement, and conversion Proactively identify and manage delivery risks, dependencies, and trade-offs in a complex, matrixed environment What We’re Looking For 10+ years’ experience in digital product management, digital programme leadership, or equivalent roles—comfortable owning both the “what” and the “when/how” of delivery Proven track record delivering consumer-facing digital products , ideally mobile apps or complex digital platforms, with clear evidence of measurable outcomes Experience operating in environments with multiple stakeholders, vendors, and delivery partners—able to align teams, manage dependencies,

Certified in Architecture Frameworks; TOGAF, FEAF, DOD, Zachman. Certified Program/Project Management Professional. Deep knowledge of business ecosystems, SaaS, infrastructure as a service (IaaS), platform as a service (PaaS), SOA, APIs, open data, microservices, event-driven IT and predictive analytics. Deep understanding of information management practices, system development life cycle management, IT services management, agile and lean methodologies, infrastructure and operations, and EA and ITIL frameworks. review.jd_text: Role Overview (MENA) The Director of Technology Operations - MENA is a senior regional leadership role, accountable for technology operations, service delivery, and operational excellence across the Middle East and North Africa. The role supports companys' rapidly growing MENA client base—particularly regulated alternative investment firms—while aligning with global standards, platforms, and governance defined by the CTO organization. review.jd_text: CTO – Dubai – UAE-N/Arabic Speaker Key Responsibilities Define and drive the company’s technology strategy, ensuring alignment with overall business objectives while identifying opportunities for innovation and operational efficiency. Lead the engineering function with a hands-on approach, including conducting architecture and code reviews, establishing technical standards, and making key build vs. buy decisions. Own and evolve the end-to-end technical architecture of the company’s products and platforms, including system design, technology stack selection, data architecture, and engineering best practices. Design, implement, and oversee robust security architecture across all products, including threat modeling, secure SDLC practices, penetration testing, and incident response planning. Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, leading research and development initiatives to evaluate and adopt new solutions that support business growth. Evaluate, select, and manage technology vendors and partners, including contract negotiation and performance management to ensure optimal value delivery. Oversee the technology budget, ensuring effective allocation of resources across strategic and operational initiatives while identifying cost optimization opportunities. Collaborate closely with executive leadership and cross-functional teams to align technology initiatives with business priorities, ensuring clear communication and stakeholder engagement. Ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards related to technology, data privacy, and information security, implementing policies to mitigate risk and maintain governance. Strong ability to conduct code reviews and assess software architecture, ensuring scalability, performance, and maintainability. Proven experience working with cloud-native architectures (AWS, Azure, or GCP), along with containerization technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. Experience designing and developing APIs, microservices, and distributed systems in complex environments. Solid understanding of modern security engineering practices, extending beyond governance into implementation. Hands-on proficiency in at least one modern programming language, such as Python, Go, Java, or TypeScript. Candidates must have recent, practical experience in software development or system architecture within the last 5 years. Profiles lacking this will not be considered. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; a Master’s degree is preferred. 20+ years of experience in senior technology leadership roles, with substantial hands-on expertise in software development and system architecture. Proven track record of driving technology innovation, including the design, development, and successful implementation of scalable solutions. Strong lead

Responsible for achieving quarterly & annual Product sales targets by collaborating with partners. Responsible for building business plans for new business jointly with partners Demonstrates market knowledge including various solutions, markets and competitive intelligence Developing and managing sales cycles from lead to closure. Responsible for building and maintaining sales pipeline in CRM, forecasting accuracy is key Conduct regular reviews and provide feedback to partners Recruit and On-Board New Partners. Also work with existing Channel & Resell partners in UAE to grow Indirect business Building and developing market plans for new business development. Lead generation and direct outreach to customers for business development Comfortable to set-up meetings and present to CXO’s Qualifying sales opportunities to ensure optimal use of Pre-sales & consulting resources time during the sales cycle and demonstrating leadership throughout the cycle Working with marketing and local lead Generation agencies to help develop lead generation campaigns along with target marketing to specific verticals. Evaluate partner pipeline authenticity. This can be done if candidate has a good network and knowledge of the local market Maintain a healthy and professional relationship with existing partners. Should always look for opportunities to co-sell ERP in industries where partner specialises. These could be new industries that need core ERP modules Drive partner enablement and have their employees access LMS and complete courses. Bachelor’s degree with a minimum of 8 years of experience in ERP Software/Solution Sales with minimum 8 years of regional experience. review.title: Managing Director, UAE
review.jd_text: As a global leader in digital, compliance-led collections, InDebted partners with the world’s most innovative enterprises to modernise recovery, protect brand equity, and transform the customer experience. We are now bringing that model to the UAE through a new joint venture anchored by ADCB — and we are hiring a Managing Director to lead and scale our presence in one of our most important new markets. This is a senior, high-impact leadership role responsible for owning the UAE P&L, driving enterprise growth across banks/telcos/utilities/BNPLs, and establishing InDebted as the region’s benchmark for compliant, digital-first collections. About The Role The Managing Director (UAE) is the senior-most leadership position for the market, accountable for commercial growth, operational performance, regulatory alignment, and long-term client relationships. You will lead the UAE go-to-market strategy, spearhead expansion beyond ADCB, and ensure we execute a relationship-driven enterprise entry model suited to the UAE market’s dynamics. You will build, lead, and manage a small but high-performing local team — including hiring and managing the Business Development Lead — while working closely with global product, compliance, operations, and executive leadership. What You’ll Do Define and execute the UAE market strategy, calibrated for a traditional-industry client mix and longer enterprise procurement cycles. Own full P&L responsibility for the UAE market, including revenue, costs, and profitability targets. Accelerate growth through deep relationships across banks, telcos, utilities, BNPLs, and other regulated lenders. Serve as the senior enterprise face of InDebted in the region — building trust with C-level leaders, regulators, and strategic partners. Lead development of a high-quality commercial pipeline beyond ADCB through your existing network, targeted enterprise outreach, and relationship-based selling. Oversee the activation, onboarding, and performance of ADCB as the anchor client, ensuring strong delivery, compliance, and satisfaction. Hire, develop, and manage the local BD Lead and future UAE team members. Ensure regulatory alignment across data sovereignty, digital collections standards, and client-specific compliance requirements. Partner with global product and operations to ensure seamless service delivery and continuous improvement for UAE clients. Report market insights, commercial progress, pipeline health, forecast performance, and operational KPIs to global leadership and JV stakeholders. What You'll Need 10+ years of leadership experience in collections, financial services, BNPL, telco credit, or enterprise B2B environments in the UAE. Demonstrated success in selling and delivering enterprise services to banks, telcos, or other regulated institutions.A strong, established network across senior credit, collections, risk, and operations leaders in the UAE — essential for accelerating early pipeline. Experience owning a P&L, m
